title: 10 best herbal tinctures for jaw lock

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with jaw lock because they promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension. The herb's natural sedative properties can calm an overactive nervous system, which is often linked to bruxism or teeth grinding. By relaxing the muscles of the face and jaw, valerian tincture can help alleviate the pressure and discomfort associated with a locked jaw, allowing for smoother, more efficient chewing and swallowing. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce pain and swelling in the jaw and surrounding areas.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Valerian root tincture helps to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can contribute to jaw locking (also known as temporomandibular joint disorder or TMD).
Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian's muscle-relaxing compounds help to reduce muscle spasms and tension in the face, jaw, and neck, which can contribute to jaw locking.
Antispasmodic properties: Valerian tincture helps to relieve spasms and contractions in the muscles of mastication (chewing), allowing for a more relaxed and pain-free jaw function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valerian have a high concentration of essential oils, which are responsible for its sedative and relaxing properties, making it effective in treating jaw lock.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes contain a higher amount of valerenic acid, a key compound that has been shown to reduce anxiety and tension, often associated with jaw locking.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valerian root valeriana officinalis by digging up clean roots in early morning or late afternoon.
Wash the harvested roots thoroughly with cool running water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces using scissors or a knife for easy extraction.
Combine one part of chopped valerian root with four parts of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for eight to ten weeks with occasional shaking before straining and bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with jaw lock because of its unique ability to relax the muscles in the jaw, face, and neck. The herb contains a compound called passionflower, which has a sedative effect on the nervous system, calming down tension and reducing stress-induced clenching. By soothing these areas, maypop tincture can alleviate symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, such as jaw pain, stiffness, and clicking or popping sounds when opening or closing the mouth.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Maypop's antispasmodic compounds help to relax and calm muscle spasms in the jaw, reducing tension and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint disorders (TMJ).
Sedative effects: The tincture's sedative properties can help to reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers of jaw clenching and locking, by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Maypop's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw joint, which can contribute to pain and stiffness associated with jaw locks.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant contain compounds that help to relax the muscles in the jaw, reducing tension and pain associated with jaw locking.
Fruits: The fruits of the Maypop plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and soothe the joints and tissues affected by jaw locking.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50 grams of dried maypop passionflower flowers and leaves and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Combine 1000 milliliters of vodka or grain alcohol with the 50 grams of dried herbs in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily during that time frame.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to smaller dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in the refrigerator for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with jaw lock because of its ability to calm and relax the nervous system, which is often linked to tension in the face and jaw. The herb's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, allowing for a more relaxed state that doesn't contribute to bruxism or teeth grinding. Additionally, Brahmi's natural anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe and calm the jaw muscles, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
remedy_properties: Memory-enhancing properties: Brahmi's ability to improve memory and cognitive function can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related jaw locking, as reduced mental tension can contribute to relaxed jaw muscles.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Brahmi's anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, which are often linked to jaw locking.

Neuroprotective properties: Brahmi's neuroprotective compounds can help calm nervous tension and promote relaxation in the muscles of the face and jaw, reducing muscle spasms that may contribute to jaw locking.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Brahmi are rich in bioactive compounds like brahmoside and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and relax muscle contractions in the jaw.

Rhyzomes: The underground stem (rhyzome) of Brahmi contains a high concentration of alkaloids like bacopasaponin, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective for treating jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and stems in the morning when dew is still present.
Rinse the harvested plant material under cold running water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Chop the plant material into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife quickly and precisely.
Steep 100g of chopped bacopa monnieri in 500ml of organic vodka for 6-8 hours at room temperature.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into dark glass bottles discarding solids completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with jaw lock because of its calming and relaxing properties, which can soothe muscle tension in the face and neck. The tincture's sedative effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for jaw locking. Additionally, lavender's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds may also contribute to its pain-relieving properties, providing welcome relief from the discomfort associated with locked jaws. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension, English lavender tinctures offer a natural solution for alleviating jaw lock and related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic Properties: English lavender's antispasmodic properties can help relax tense muscles and reduce spasms in the jaw, neck, and face, alleviating tension headaches and jaw locking.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected areas, making it an effective natural remedy for reducing jaw pain and stiffness associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: English lavender's anxiolytic and sedative properties can help calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress that often exacerbate jaw locking and tension headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their calming properties, making them effective in soothing jaw tension and relaxing the muscles.
Flowers: Lavender flowers contain a high concentration of essential oils with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried english lavender flowers in the morning when they are most potent and fragrant.
Combine one part dried english lavender flowers with four parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the alcohol slowly.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the liquid tincture.
Transfer the final tincture into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool place for up to two years for optimal potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with jaw lock because they contain a unique compound called kavalactones, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. When consumed, these compounds help to relax the muscles of the face and jaw, reducing tension and stiffness associated with bruxism (teeth grinding) and temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. As a result, herbal kava tinctures can provide relief from jaw lock, allowing individuals to speak, eat, and sleep more comfortably without the discomfort and pain caused by tense facial muscles.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Kava's anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) effects help to reduce muscle tension in the jaw, which can contribute to jaw locking or clenching.

Muscle relaxant properties: The active compounds in kava tincture, such as kavalactones, have a relaxing effect on muscles, which can help to alleviate spasms and tension in the jaw, neck, and face that may lead to jaw lock.

Pain-relieving properties: Kava's pain-relieving effects can help to reduce or eliminate the discomfort associated with jaw locking or temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, making it easier to open one's mouth without pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the kava plant have been traditionally used to treat anxiety and stress-related conditions, including jaw lock.
Rhyzomes: Kava rhyzomes contain a high concentration of active compounds that help to relax muscles and relieve tension in the face, making them effective for treating jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry kava root: collect fresh piper methysticum roots, wash them thoroughly, and dry them in the shade or using a food dehydrator for at least 24 hours.
Chop and grind kava root: use scissors or a sharp knife to chop the dried kava roots into small pieces, then grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix with glycerin and water: combine 100 grams of powdered kava root with 200 milliliters of glycerin and 50 milliliters of distilled water in a clean glass jar to create a tincture base.

Steep and macerate the mixture: allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily, before straining it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar.

Fill and store the finished tincture: transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, label them clearly, and store them in the refrigerator for up to 2 years to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with jaw lock because it contains compounds that have a profound impact on the muscles of the face, particularly those involved in jaw movement. The tannins and flavonoids present in the tincture work to relax and soothe tense muscle fibers, reducing spasms and tension that can lead to jaw lock. As the muscles release their grip, the jaw is able to move freely once again, alleviating discomfort and pain. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective way to address jaw lock and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: The tincture relaxes muscle spasms and cramps, including those in the jaw muscles, to alleviate tension and pain associated with jaw lock.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Maiden's Hair Tree Tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to jaw lock.

Antioxidant properties: The tincture's antioxidants help protect the jaw muscles and joints from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate jaw lock and other temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are good for making medicinal tinctures for jaw lock as they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw.
Barks: The barks are used due to their ability to stimulate blood flow and relax muscles, which can help alleviate jaw tension and lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the morning after dew has dried ensuring proper identification and cleanliness.
Cleanly dry ginkgo biloba leaves using a food dehydrator on low setting for 6 hours or air-drying method for 24 hours.
Combine 1 part dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 5 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar storing in a dark place for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding solids and setting aside liquid tincture.
Store tincture in dark glass bottles at room temperature or refrigerate using within 6 months of preparation and taking 20-30 drops per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime tinctures helps with jaw lock because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds that can reduce muscle tension and relax the jaw, thereby alleviating symptoms of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder. Additionally, the tincture's ability to soothe irritated tissues and reduce inflammation in the face and neck area can help to release physical and emotional tension, allowing for a more comfortable and relaxed jaw and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Broad-leaved lime tincture helps to reduce inflammation in the jaw and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to jaw lock by reducing swelling and discomfort.
Pain-relieving properties: The tincture's analgesic properties help to numb pain in the jaw, allowing for temporary relief from tension and stiffness that can lead to jaw lock.
Muscle relaxant properties: Broad-leaved lime contains compounds that help to relax tense muscles, including those in the jaw, which can reduce muscle spasms and strain that contribute to jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the broad-leaved lime plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to relieve toothaches and tension-related jaw pain.
Barks: The barks of the broad-leaved lime plant contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms, making them effective for treating jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves from a trusted source or dried leaves in bulk.
Chop 10-20% of the total weight in dried leaves into small pieces for optimal extraction.
Mix chopped leaves with 60-70% proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:2.
Store the mixture in a dark, cool place for 6-8 weeks to allow for slow extraction.
Strain and filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops tinctures helps with jaw lock because of its natural ability to relax the muscles in the face, particularly those responsible for tension and stiffness in the jaw. The antispasmodic properties of hops help to calm and soothe the jaw joint, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with temporomandibular disorders (TMD). Additionally, the calming effects of hops can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are common triggers for jaw locking. By using an herbal hops tincture, individuals may experience relief from chronic jaw tension and improved overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Hops tincture's antispasmodic properties can help to relax and relieve muscle tension in the jaw, reducing lockjaw symptoms.
Anxiolytic and sedative properties: The calming effects of hops tincture can help to alleviate anxiety and stress, which are common underlying causes of bruxism (teeth grinding) and jaw locking.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Hops' anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw locking, as well as inflammation in the surrounding tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hops plant have been traditionally used in herbal remedies due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving tension and pain associated with jaw lock.
Buds: Hops buds contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from jaw pain and tension caused by temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh humulus lupulus leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Rinse the harvested material with clean water then pat it dry with paper towels immediately.
Combine 100 grams of dried humulus lupulus leaves and stems with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or brandy in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six weeks to eight weeks allowing for maceration and extraction.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marijuana tinctures helps with jaw lock because they contain cannabidiol, a non-psychoactive compound that targets the trigeminal nerve, which is responsible for facial and jaw muscle contractions. The tincture's bioavailable CBD absorbs quickly into the bloodstream, calming muscle spasms and tension in the jaw, neck, and face. This natural relief from TMJ disorder and teeth grinding can bring instant comfort to those suffering from jaw lock, allowing them to chew, eat, and sleep with ease once again.
remedy_properties: Cannabidiol (CBD): Helps to reduce inflammation and pain associated with temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ) by interacting with the body's endocannabinoid system, which can alleviate tension and spasms in the jaw.

Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC): Provides rapid relief from pain, anxiety, and stress, which are common contributors to jaw lock or bruxism, by activating CB1 receptors in the brain and reducing muscle contractions.

Terpenes: Specifically, myrcene, a terpene found in many herbal marijuana strains, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cannabis plant contain a high concentration of cannabidiol (CBD), which has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in relieving jaw pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes are a rich source of sesquiterpenes, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, helping to relax the muscles and joints of the face and jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cannabis sativa flowers at peak potency for optimal therapeutic effects.
Dry the harvested cannabis flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least seven days.
Combine one part dried cannabis flowers with five parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and transfer it to a dark glass bottle for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Treetop hydrangea tinctures helps with jaw lock because its unique composition of bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, has a profound impact on the musculature of the face. The tincture's soothing properties can effectively calm the jaw muscles, reducing tension and spasms that often lead to jaw locking. Additionally, hydrangea's anti-inflammatory effects may help alleviate any underlying inflammation or irritation in the joint, promoting relaxation and relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Hydrangea root has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and discomfort in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), which can contribute to jaw lock.
Antispasmodic: The tincture's antispasmodic properties may help relax muscles in the face, neck, and jaw, reducing spasms and tension that can lead to jaw lock.
Pain-relieving: Hydrangea root contains compounds that may help reduce pain and discomfort in the TMJ and surrounding areas, making it a potential natural remedy for alleviating jaw lock.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Treetop Hydrangea contain compounds that help to relax the muscles and reduce tension in the jaw, making it effective for treating jaw lock.
Barks: The barks of this plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the gums and surrounding tissues, reducing pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of dried hydrangea arborescens aerial parts in the early morning after dew has evaporated and before it gets too hot.
Combine the dried herb with 4 cups of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar leaving about an inch at the top.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks shaking the jar every other day to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Label the bottle with the date prepared and store it in a cool dark place for at least 2 months before using as a tincture to help alleviate jaw lock.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for jaw lock are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate tension and discomfort in the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and surrounding muscles. By soothing inflammation and relaxing tight muscles, these herbal remedies help to release tension in the jaw, reducing symptoms of TMJ disorders such as jaw locking, clicking, or grinding. Examples of helpful herbs include willow bark, which reduces pain and inflammation, and passionflower, which calms nervous tension and promotes relaxation. For individuals struggling with chronic jaw pain, herbal tinctures can significantly improve their quality of life by allowing for comfortable eating, speaking, and even sleeping.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaw lock with herbal tinctures is to combine a few carefully selected herbs that target specific areas of tension and discomfort. Try combining Valerian root, which relaxes the muscles, with Lavender, which calms the nervous system, and Peppermint, which cools and soothes the skin. Mix these tinctures in equal parts and apply topically to the affected area using a cotton swab or dropper.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw lock that are treated with herbal tinctures are various conditions characterized by muscle spasms, tension, and pain. These include headaches such as migraines and tension headaches, temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, and fibromyalgia. Herbal tinctures containing ingredients like skullcap, passionflower, and lavender can help relax muscles, reduce inflammation, and alleviate discomfort associated with these conditions. Additionally, tinctures made from valerian root, chamomile, and ginger may also provide relief from insomnia, anxiety, and digestive issues that can contribute to jaw lock.
